message: Gate validation runner tokenizes commands with strings.Fields (quote-blind), so the contract's quoted rg validation command executes as pattern '"review' with the remaining pattern fragments treated as nonexistent paths. rg exits 2 every time, the gate permanently reports failed for this contract, and review approval is blocked. The intended forbidden-phrase check never actually ran.
evidence: gate/validation/command_003/stderr.log: 'rg: loop|review: No such file or directory'; exit_code 2 in gate-report.json; gate.go:535 'fields := strings.Fields(commandText)'; gate.go is not in the change's diff (pre-existing).

message: Gate validation command_003 never tested the contract's intent: the gate splits commands with strings.Fields (no shell-quote handling), so the quoted rg pattern fragmented — rg searched for the literal pattern '"review' and treated 'loop|review', 'prepare|clarify', 'suggest|review_not_prepared|project_map_stale"' as file paths, exiting 2 with 'No such file or directory'. Independently, the command's intent is zero matches, but rg exits 1 on zero matches and the gate treats nonzero exit as failure — so this command structurally fails on a clean tree. The run cannot converge through a passing gate with this command as written; replace it (e.g. invert the exit code) or accept the gate manually.
evidence: gate/validation/command_003/stderr.log shows 'rg: loop|review: No such file or directory (os error 2)' etc.; gate.go:535 'fields := strings.Fields(commandText)'; gate.go is not in the change's diff (pre-existing runner behavior).

message: Removed-grammar phrases remain in docs outside the test guards: docs/real-agent-execution-dogfood.md lines 65/119/122 still name 'pactum review prepare' and 'review prepare' (file untouched by this change), and docs/backlog.md lines 154/190/379/559/562 keep 'clarify suggest' plus 'review loop' concept mentions (file edited by this change, mentions kept as historical record). The contract's rg validation sweeps all of docs and contract assumption 3 expects a human review of remaining matches — which never happened because command_003 misfired. Human should accept these as intentional historical fixtures or scope the validation command to the guarded doc set.
evidence: rg 'review loop|review prepare|clarify suggest|review_not_prepared|project_map_stale' over docs matches these lines; docs_test.go scans only requiredDocFiles (README, SECURITY, install, flow, workspace, agents, memory); dogfood doc absent from the gate's changed-files list.

message: Acceptance criterion 'review finding resolve, proposal accept/reject, and review fix run/apply still require relevant existing review records' has no test at the new boundary. requireReviewPrepared was removed from these paths and the old *BeforeReviewPreparedFails tests were deleted without replacements that run these commands on a gated run with no review artifacts. All current tests pre-scaffold via scaffoldReviewForTest, so nothing pins that these commands fail with ordinary missing-record errors and do not self-scaffold review/review.json; switching them to ensureReviewRecord would regress the criterion with tests green.
evidence: review.go:483, review_proposals.go:214/303, review_fix.go:196 define the new missing-record boundary; git diff shows TestReviewProposeFindingsBeforeReviewPreparedFails et al. removed; grep finds no test asserting 'review finding not found'/'review proposal not found'/'no review findings found' on an unscaffolded run.

message: Four new hand-written CLI error paths in clarifyAnswerCmd.Run are untested: the --recommended/--all-recommended conflict (line 69), the --all-recommended usage string (line 73), the --recommended usage string (line 83), and the q_ prefix check in the --recommended branch (line 87). TestUsageErrorsAdvertiseNewGrammar (cli_grammar_test.go:169) exists to pin every hand-written usage-error string but was not extended with the new strings.
evidence: grep over internal/app/*_test.go finds no occurrence of 'not both', 'usage: pactum clarify answer [run_id] --all-recommended', or the --recommended usage string; the grammar test table only contains the typed-answer usage.

message: Constant reviewerLastResultArtifact is dead code: its only consumer (writeReviewRun, deleted with the old reviewer-only ReviewRun) is gone. Unused package-level constants are not caught by go vet or the deadcode gate, so it will linger silently. Delete it.
evidence: rg across internal/ finds only the declaration; at HEAD it was used once, in the deleted writeReviewRun (old review.go:1531).

message: Constant clarifierLastResultArtifact is dead code: its only consumer (writeClarifySuggest, deleted when clarify suggest was removed and the round primitive became JSON-only) is gone. Delete it.
evidence: rg across internal/ finds only the declaration; at HEAD it was used once, in the deleted writeClarifySuggest (old clarify_suggest.go:683).

message: README quick-start says 'Mutating review commands self-scaffold the review record once the gate report exists', but only review run, review finding add, and review approve scaffold (ensureReviewRecord call sites in internal/app/review.go); review finding resolve — shown one line below — and proposal accept/reject require existing records per the contract's q_001 answer and acceptance criteria. docs/flow.md:197-199 states the precise list; the README contradicts it.
evidence: ensureReviewRecord (writes scaffold) called only from review_loop.go:158, review.go:407 (finding add), review.go:530 (approve); review.go:489 (finding resolve) uses loadOrDeriveReviewDocument which 'writes nothing' per its comment at review.go:367-370.

message: The agent-skill workflow reference says 'the mutating review commands self-scaffold the review record' and then lists review finding resolve among them; resolve does not scaffold and requires existing records. Agents act on this file, so the overgeneralization can mislead an agent into expecting resolve/proposal-accept to work on a gated run with no review data.
evidence: Same code evidence as README finding: only review run / finding add / approve call ensureReviewRecord; contract acceptance criterion explicitly keeps review finding resolve and proposal accept/reject on ordinary missing-record errors.

message: prompt build human output still prints `pactum execute plan --agent codex` without the run id, while the final affordance surface requires concrete runnable Next commands such as `pactum execute plan <run_id> --agent codex`.
evidence: writePromptBuild prints the run-id-less command; nextCommandsForRun emits `pactum execute plan ` + runID + ` --agent codex`, and the affordance test still asserts the old human output.

message: `clarify run` still routes its only internal clarifier round through a stdout JSON serialization/unmarshal adapter after removing the user-facing `clarify suggest` command.
evidence: `runClarifyLoopSuggestRound` buffers `runClarifierRound` JSON output and unmarshals it; `runClarifierRound` hardcodes `JSONOutput: true` and has unreachable render callbacks solely to satisfy the generic lifecycle.
